senses.definition: plural of premandibular; not a standard common English term, likely a technical or coined word referring to something located before the mandible (lower jaw) in anatomy or a specific speculative concept.
examples: The study referenced several premandibulars in the comparative anatomy section.
Researchers debated the role of premandibulars in early vertebrate specimens.

senses.definition: the period before becoming a man; a nonstandard/archaic style referring to early or pre-adulthood in a male context.
examples: He spoke of his premanhood with a sense of nostalgia.
The club explored themes of identity in premanhood.

senses.definition: having a tendency toward or characteristic of mania before the onset of full mania; used descriptively in psychology or informal speech.
examples: The patient showed premaniacal behaviors that worried the clinicians.
Her premaniacal energy dwindled as stress increased.

senses.definition: preliminary or before the manifestation of a disease, symptom, or feature.
examples: She showed premanifest signs that suggested an upcoming illness.
The trial looked for premanifest indicators in participants.

senses.definition: the phase or state before a manifest appearance or expression; something that exists before a notable event or symptom.
examples: Researchers studied premanifestation risks in the cohort.
The data highlighted several premanifestation patterns.

senses.definition: the human species before the present form or as a prehistoric or mythic past.
examples: In the story, premankind faced a different moral landscape.
Scholars debate the traits of premankind in ancient texts.

senses.definition: to produce or prepare something before the main manufacturing process; to assemble preliminary components.
examples: They premanufactured the panels off-site.
Premanufacture several modules to speed assembly.

senses.definition: manufactured beforehand; produced prior to final assembly or use.
examples: Premanufactured components arrived on site.
The premanufactured units simplified the construction timeline.

senses.definition: a person or company that premanufactures components; a supplier of prebuilt parts.
examples: The premaufacturer supplied modular walls.
As a premanufacturer, they focus on off-site production.

senses.definition: third-person singular of premanufacture; produces components beforehand.
examples: The firm premanufactures modular units for quick assembly.
They premanufactures parts for several projects.

senses.definition: the stage of producing components before final assembly; off-site production.
examples: Premanufacturing reduces on-site construction time.
We reviewed the premaufacturing schedule.

senses.definition: before marriage; related to the period before wedding.
examples: They attended premarital counseling.
Premarital agreements can clarify finances before marriage.

senses.definition: in a premarital manner; before marriage.
examples: They lived premaritally for several years.
The couple chose premaritally to keep their finances separate.

senses.definition: the period before a market opens; a time when trading is not yet active.
examples: Investors watched the stock indices during premarket hours.
Premarket analysts release data ahead of the opening bell.

senses.definition: before the market opens; relating to activities that occur before trading begins.
examples: Premarket trading can be volatile.
They issued a premaket guidance update.

senses.definition: advertised or marketed before the official market launch.
examples: The product was premarketed to select retailers.
Premarketed items boosted early interest.

senses.definition: the phase of marketing before a product is released; promotional activities ahead of launch.
examples: Premarketing built anticipation for the new device.
They invested in premarketing to gauge demand.

senses.definition: plural of premarketing; promotional activities before a product launch.
examples: The team analyzed several premarketings to refine the message.
Premarketings informed the final launch plan.

senses.definition: plural of premarket; times or contexts before market open; or premarket items.
examples: Investors monitored the premarkets before the official open.
Premarkets showed unusual activity.

senses.definition: the period before marriage; before getting married.
examples: They attended premarriage counseling.
The premarriage agreement addressed finances.

senses.definition: plural of premarriage; periods or events before marriages.
examples: The survey studied premarriages among young couples.
Premarriages can involve planning and negotiations.

senses.definition: married before a certain time or immediately as pre-existing; in some contexts used humorously.
examples: Premarried friends shared stories from their early years.
They were premarried to their partners in the project.

senses.definition: to marry before a certain time or soon; to enter into marriage.
examples: They plan to premarry next year.
The couple decided to premarry after completing their degrees.

senses.definition: the act of marrying before an expected time; engaging in marriage early.
examples: They are premarrying to ensure legal benefits.
Premarrying can affect financial planning.

senses.definition: the state of being before mastery; early stage of learning.
examples: She reached premastery in the skill after months of practice.
Premastery indicates foundational understanding.

senses.definition: an event or activity that occurs before a main match or competition.
examples: The teams warmed up during the prematch.
Promoters scheduled a prematch press conference.
